context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=10; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit, dessert, or another cold-served item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, or task-focused THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold items were explicitly preferred without reheating across contexts. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, potatoes, or hash browns THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected substantial reheating for observed hot meals in attention-occupied contexts. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV, including when the TV is in front or on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, hash browns, or bite-sized sandwiches THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user consistently preferred thoroughly reheated hot meals in TV contexts. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal with dipping sauce such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup THEN 2 min BECAUSE observed social-front evidence shows a somewhat moderated reheating preference relative to the stronger 3 min pattern in solitary screen-focused contexts.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a front-facing focal activity such as watching TV, working on a laptop, or interacting with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred preserving the front view in front-focused contexts, and the new laptop-left meal still selected front occlusion minimization rather than left, suggesting the main concern is protecting the central workspace or line of sight. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the right such as watching TV on the right or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly selected preserving the right-side view in multiple right-focused contexts. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the left such as watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE observed left-TV evidence showed a context-matched preference for protecting the left-side view.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN slow BECAUSE the user has usually selected the slower movement option, fitting a comfort-first interaction style. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a simple cold fruit meal THEN medium BECAUSE the new laptop-fruit episode shows a modest increase in speed, consistent with tolerating somewhat faster motion for a straightforward meal while occupied. Across observed evidence, the tie-break remains comfort-first, with medium appearing as a context-specific efficiency adjustment rather than a global shift.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition such as fruit pieces, strawberries, broccoli, or hot solid foods including chicken dishes AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused work contexts, or TV contexts THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option across several observed feeding meals, now including mixed fruit in a laptop context. IF food is a long, narrow, elongated, or hand-food-like solid such as chicken strips, bite-sized sandwiches, or chicken nuggets AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is entertainment-focused or social THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated selections with elongated or nugget-like foods indicate that food shape can override the general preference for acquisition success.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted explicit web confirmation in social settings, indicating a reliability preference despite extra interaction time. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop or entertainment-focused with straightforward fork-fed food such as strawberries, mixed fruit, bite-sized sandwiches, or simple hot solids AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is straightforward fork-fed food THEN no BECAUSE the user selected skipping confirmation in several divided-attention contexts, consistent with preferring less interruption. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more substantial hot savory fork-fed meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN yes BECAUSE observed TV evidence indicates that for some heavier hot meals the user can favor reliability over reduced interaction even in an entertainment context. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is reliability in social settings and some substantial hot-meal situations, but lower interaction burden for simpler foods in passive or occupied settings.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward due to limited trunk control, and they consistently selected inside-mouth transfer across observed meals.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user consistently selected inside-mouth transfer and their physical profile supports that mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal readiness cue across many observed contexts, consistent with reducing conversational disruption and unnecessary audio. IF dining context is watching TV with the focal activity in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN speech + LED has also been observed BECAUSE one prior front-TV meal showed a preference for extra salience, but repeated evidence across contexts favors LED as the more stable tendency.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and repeatedly selected open-mouth initiation in social meals, favoring explicit user-driven readiness over automatic progression when another person is present. IF feeding task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred more automation when attention was occupied. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is toward automation under divided attention, while social settings favor explicit but still low-effort open-mouth signaling.

IF drinking task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for drinking in observed social meals. IF drinking task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for wiping in observed social meals. IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal cue when the tool reached transfer position, suggesting a stable preference for quiet visual-only completion signaling. IF dining context is watching TV with the focal activity in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN speech + LED has also been observed BECAUSE one prior front-TV meal showed a preference for a more salient multimodal completion cue, but repeated evidence now favors LED as the more stable choice.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, which fits their inability to reliably press buttons.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal activity that the user wants to keep visible, including in front, to the right, or to the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in social, laptop-working, and TV contexts, and the new laptop-left meal continued this pattern.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred limited dipping in social meals, consistent with reducing messiness around other people. IF food includes a dip or sauce or topping AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping or extra coating in observed laptop meals, suggesting a stronger mess-avoidance preference when occupied. IF food has no dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable and observed no-sauce meals, including the new fruit meal, also used do-not-dip.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a moderate wait in social meals, balancing efficiency with enough time to respond using assistive interaction methods. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec for strongly attention-capturing work contexts, but 100 sec has also been observed for a simpler cold-fruit laptop meal BECAUSE the user usually prefers a long intervention window while working, yet the new evidence shows a shorter moderate wait can be acceptable when the meal is straightforward and lower risk. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec when the TV is directly in front, but 100 sec has also been observed in other TV contexts BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals indicate the user wants substantially more time to intervene when attention is strongly captured by a front-facing screen, whereas other TV contexts tolerated a moderate wait. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is to allow more time under stronger distraction, with 100 sec remaining the general baseline.
